svg.title: Mini key-value store architecture
svg.desc: Bird's eye view of a mini KV store: clients connect to a TCP listener, which spawns connection handlers, which talk to a shared in-memory store, which optionally persists to an AOF log on disk.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What commands do clients send to the KV store?')
sendPrompt('Can multiple clients connect at the same time?')
sendPrompt('How does the client send a command like SET foo bar?')
sendPrompt('How does the TCP listener work in Rust with tokio?')
sendPrompt('What does a connection handler do in a KV store?')
sendPrompt('What does a connection handler do in a KV store?')
sendPrompt('What does a connection handler do in a KV store?')
sendPrompt('How do you protect the HashMap with Arc and Mutex in Rust?')
sendPrompt('How does AOF persistence work in a KV store?')
sendPrompt('How do you implement TTL expiry in a Rust KV store?')
